title: Java Optional Lazy Fallback
description: Practice choosing orElseGet when fallback work should be lazy.
learningGoals: Use orElseGet to defer fallback work until an Optional is empty.
Recognize that orElse evaluates its argument before Optional decides which value to return.

completionChecks.prompt: What does this code print when nickname contains "Ada"?
choices: It fails to compile because Supplier cannot be passed to orElseGet
completionChecks.explanation: The Optional contains Ada and the filter keeps it, so orElseGet returns the present value without calling the fallback supplier.

completionChecks.prompt: What changes if the final call uses orElse(buildGuestName()) instead?
choices: buildGuestName runs even when nickname already contains Ada
The code returns Optional<String> instead of String
The fallback becomes lazy in the same way as orElseGet
The filter is skipped before the fallback is evaluated
completionChecks.explanation: Method arguments are evaluated before the method call, so orElse(buildGuestName()) performs the fallback work even when the Optional value is present.

completionChecks.prompt: What does the code print if nickname is Optional.of("Al") instead of Optional.of("Ada")?
choices: It throws NoSuchElementException when the filter rejects the name
completionChecks.explanation: The trap is forgetting that filter can empty a present Optional; orElseGet then calls the supplier and returns guest.
